region_of_origin: East Asia — Japan, China, Korea; widely available in Singapore at wet markets and NTUC FairPrice
food_subgroup: Mushrooms (Fungi) — fresh, caps and stems, raw, as sold

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for fresh shiitake mushroom as a food product; SNOMED CT coverage for culinary fungi is limited.

serving.description: 100g fresh shiitake mushrooms (caps and stems, as sold)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard 100g serving of fresh shiitake mushrooms (approximately 4–6 medium caps with stems) as sold at Singapore wet markets and supermarkets.

nutrient_source.title: Mushrooms, Shiitake, Raw — USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 168436)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Direct match to USDA SR Legacy entry for raw shiitake mushroom. All per-100g nutrient values sourced from FDC ID 168436. Vitamin D value (0.4 mcg) reflects baseline indoor-grown fresh shiitake; UV-activated values are not included in the per_100g table as they are preparation-dependent.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mushrooms — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~15 referenced from University of Sydney GI Database for shiitake mushroom. Available carbohydrate calculated as total carbohydrate (6.8g) minus dietary fibre (2.5g) = 4.3g per 100g. Glycemic load per 100g serving = (15 × 4.3) / 100 = 0.65, rounded to 0.7. GI is very low, consistent with low-starchy, high-fibre fungal food.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (9mg per 100g) and high potassium (304mg per 100g)
hypertension.guidance: Fresh shiitake mushrooms are an excellent choice for hypertensive patients: sodium content is negligible at 9mg per 100g, while potassium at 304mg supports vascular tone and counteracts sodium-mediated blood pressure elevation; consume freely as part of a DASH-aligned diet.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index (GI ~15) and low glycemic load (~0.7 per 100g serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 15 and a glycemic load of 0.7 per 100g, fresh shiitake mushrooms cause minimal postprandial glucose excursion and are suitable for inclusion in a carbohydrate-controlled diet for Type 2 Diabetes management; the high dietary fibre (2.5g) further moderates glucose absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat (0.1g), zero cholesterol, and high potassium with beta-glucan fibre
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Fresh shiitake mushrooms are cardioprotective: saturated fat is 0.1g per 100g, cholesterol is zero, and the beta-glucan (lentinan) content at culinary doses may contribute modestly to LDL cholesterol reduction; potassium (304mg) supports healthy blood pressure in cardiovascular patients.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Vitamin D content (baseline 0.4 mcg; UV-activated up to 15–40 mcg) and immunomodulatory beta-glucan
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Vitamin D deficiency is associated with higher rheumatoid arthritis disease activity; UV-activated fresh shiitake can provide a practical plant-based vitamin D source, and lentinan (beta-1,3-glucan) has mild immunomodulatory properties at culinary doses.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Shiitake Mushrooms (Fresh) — raw, caps and stems, as sold at Singapore wet markets and NTUC FairPrice. Per 100g: 34 kcal, 2.2g protein, 0.5g fat, 6.8g carbohydrate, 2.5g dietary fibre, 9mg sodium, 304mg potassium. Glycemic index ~15 (very low); glycemic load ~0.7 per 100g serving. Texture: firm, meaty caps with white stems; mild earthy umami flavour when raw, deepening to rich savoury on cooking. Culinary uses: stir-fries, soups, hot pots, braised dishes, and as a flavour base in East Asian cooking. Origin: Lentinula edodes, native to East Asia (Japan, China, Korea); widely cultivated and available fresh in Singapore. Nutritional highlights: excellent source of B vitamins — riboflavin (0.22mg), niacin (3.9mg), vitamin B6 (0.29mg); meaningful potassium (304mg) and phosphorus (112mg); selenium (5.7 mcg). Baseline vitamin D: 0.4 mcg per 100g (indoor-grown). UV ACTIVATION: exposing fresh shiitake gill-side up to direct sunlight for 15–60 minutes can raise vitamin D2 content to 15–40 mcg per 100g (600–1,600 IU), exceeding the daily RDA of 15 mcg — making UV-activated shiitake one of the most practical plant-based vitamin D sources for vegetarians and vegans in Singapore. Sun-dried shiitake can contain 24–50 mcg vitamin D2. Dietary flags: vegan, vegetarian, halal, gluten-free, dairy-free. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC 168436.
common_user_queries: how to get vitamin D from mushrooms
vegan vitamin D food sources Singapore

confidence_reason: All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 168436 (raw shiitake mushroom), an approved primary source with full macro and micronutrient coverage. GI value referenced from University of Sydney GI Database. Ontology codes (FoodOn, FoodEx2) are verified direct matches. No estimation or analogy required for core nutritional data.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for raw shiitake mushroom is not reported in USDA SR Legacy FDC 168436. water_g set to -1: moisture content not extracted from source for this record; USDA reports approximately 89.7g water per 100g for raw shiitake but this was not included in the input record and is noted here for reference only. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FDC 168436). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — fungi do not synthesise cholesterol). vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FDC 168436 — shiitake contains no retinol or beta-carotene). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FDC 168436). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — plant/fungal food contains no B12). vitamin_e_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FDC 168436). Vitamin D value of 0.4 mcg reflects baseline indoor-grown fresh shiitake; UV-activated values (15–40 mcg per 100g) are preparation-dependent and not included in the per_100g table. GI source is University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate basis documented in gi_source). This is a generic fresh ingredient reference record; it does not represent any specific cooked preparation.
